author: y4rr
content:
Decision A: Implemented real save-by-index (not fail-fast). Refactored interpreter into shared interpret_collect_values + extract_outputs helpers, added pub fn interpret_multi_with_captures(graph, inputs, capture_topo_indices) -> (outputs, captured). forward_with_ctx now calls it with self.saved_tensor_indices and stores the result. Honest contract: each entry in saved_tensors is the first output of the node at the corresponding saved_tensor_indices topo position. Also exposed pub fn saved_tensors() so tests can verify the contract.

Decision B: Fail-fast (not full replay). Re-executing an unsupported op against a fresh input requires lowering an unknown-by-design GradFn name into a real callable — that's the same gap that caused the graph break. The honest answer is JitError::UnsupportedOp { op: <name> }; the eager closure now returns Err with the op name baked in. The cached output_tensor capture (the silent bug) is removed.

Decision C: Split return type via new pub enum InductorCompileStatus { Compiled(CompiledGraph), FellBackToInterpreter(CompiledGraph) } plus inherent method InductorBackend::compile_with_status. The Codegen trait impl InductorBackend::compile now returns Err(JitError::UnsupportedOp { op: '...use compile_with_status...' }) for the non-fusable CpuRust path — the silent delegate to InterpreterBackend is gone. Three existing tests that exercised the silent fallback (test_inductor_compile_runs_interpreter_fallback, test_inductor_cpurust_jit_reduction_falls_back_to_interpreter, test_inductor_cpurust_jit_matmul_falls_back_to_interpreter) updated to use compile_with_status AND additionally assert backend.compile(&g).is_err() — turning them into discriminators.

Discriminating tests added:
- module::tests::test_forward_with_ctx_captures_intermediate_not_just_inputs — asserts aot.saved_tensors().len() == saved_tensor_indices.len() for sum(mul(a,b)); pre-fix saved 2 vs expected 3+.
- module::tests::test_forward_with_ctx_captured_intermediate_value_matches_interpreter — asserts the Mul intermediate [4, 10, 18] appears in saved_tensors; pre-fix the cached inputs.to_vec() never contained it.
- graph_break::tests::test_eager_segment_does_not_silently_replay_cached_output — reproduces the buggy closure inline, asserts the post-fix segment Errs with UnsupportedOp naming the op.
- graph_break::tests::test_segmented_module_eager_unsupported_op_errors_at_forward — end-to-end SegmentedModule with compiled+eager, asserts the eager error propagates rather than returning a cached tensor.
- codegen::tests::test_inductor_compile_silent_fallback_is_surfaced — pairs Neg (JIT-able) vs Matmul (fallback). compile_with_status reports Compiled vs FellBackToInterpreter; trait compile() returns Ok vs Err — proving callers can distinguish.
